title: Configuring Cloud LM Licenses
html:
Configuring Cloud LM Licenses

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication

Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedJan 27, 2023
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

To configure your Uniface application to connect to a Cloud LM Entitlement, you need to enter your Registration Token during installation or run the Uniface License Management utility (ulic) after installing Uniface. If you are using Cloud LM to license your Uniface Docker image, you can enter the registration token REGTOKEN environment variable when mounting your image.

Before you begin, you must have:

  • An internet connection to connect to the Cloud License Manager.
  • A Registration Token from your administrator. If you are an administrator, see Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing.

Configure the license when installing Uniface

To configure cloud LM licensing using the installer:

  1. When prompted to choose a license type during installation, select option 2 I have a Registration Token (Cloud LM licensing).
  2. When prompted by the License Registration screen at the end of installation, enter your Registration Token.

For more information on using the installer, see Install the Uniface Enterprise Edition on Windows or Installing Uniface on Unix and Linux.

Note: When configuring your Cloud LM licensing using the installer, $LICENSE_OPTIONS will be set automatically in your assignment file. If you are changing your license type, make sure your $LICENSE_OPTIONS does not include LM_LICENSE_FILE. LM_LOAD should be left out or set to S.

Configure the license using ulic

You can also use the Uniface License Management utility (ulic) to configure your application to use Cloud LM. This is useful for an existing Uniface installation, or when you have chosen to configure your license manually.

On Windows, you can use the Cloud LM Registration utility from the Start menu:
  1. Open Cloud LM Registration.
  2. Enter your Registration Token.
  3. Click Register.

Using the command line interact (all platforms):

  1. Run ulic /prep to create persistence data.
  2. Run ulic /reg={RegistrationToken} to register your token.

For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic).

Once the token is registered, no license file is downloaded, but an XML file is created inside the license folder sntlcloudp_configuration_unifacebv.xml.

Note: If you are changing your license type, make sure $LICENSE_OPTIONS in your assignment file does not include LM_LICENSE_FILE. LM_LOAD should be left out or set to S.

Configure the license for Docker

To use Cloud LM with Docker, use the environment variable REGTOKEN when spinning up your container. The environment variable is set using the switch -e with docker run, for example:

docker run -e "REGTOKEN=RegistrationToken"
Note: If you delete your Docker container, you will need a new Auth Token, therefore your Registration Token needs to be valid. If you are plan to regularly delete and re-create containers, you will need to maintain your Registration Tokens to ensure they remain valid. If you simply stop and restart your container, it re-uses the same Auth Token.

For more information on resolving licensing configuration errors, see Troubleshooting Cloud LM Licensing Issues.

Related Links
  • Troubleshooting Cloud LM Licensing Issues

    The following section provides resolution for the error which occurs while registering your Registration Token.

  • Troubleshooting Licensing Issues
  • Uniface Server Docker Image

    The Uniface Server Docker Image contains a Linux Uniface Server environment, including the Uniface Router and Uniface Server. The purpose of this image is to run Uniface solutions in a containerized Docker environment.
